\\n        total_steps = max(steps_per_epoch * max_epochs, 1)\\n\\n    warmup_steps = max(int(0.1 * total_steps), 1)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        if current_step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(current_step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (current_step - warmup_steps) / float(max(total_steps - warmup_steps, 1))\\n        progress = min(max(progress, 0.0), 1.0)\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With a fixed 256-step budget, constant LR underuses early optimization.
